events.title: Russian strike kills 24 in Kyiv
events.desc: Ukraine's air force reported that a significant aerial assault on Kyiv resulted in 24 fatalities. This attack is described as one of the largest since the conflict began, indicating a potential escalation in Russian tactics.

events.title: Russian forces pound Kyiv as eastern advance stalls
events.desc: Russian forces have intensified attacks on Kyiv while their ground advance in eastern Ukraine has slowed considerably. Ukraine's successful disruption campaign is hindering Russian arms and fuel supply to the front lines.

events.title: BRICS talks end without joint statement amid Iran war divisions
events.desc: Disagreements over the ongoing war involving Iran have led to the BRICS summit concluding without a joint statement. Iran's Foreign Minister urged member states to condemn actions by the US and Israel.

events.title: Israel kills seven in Lebanon amid ongoing talks
events.desc: Israeli forces conducted strikes in southern Lebanon, killing seven individuals. This occurred as Israel and Lebanon were engaged in their third round of direct talks, facilitated by the US, to discuss security and border issues.

events.title: Meta faces lawsuit over scam ads
events.desc: Santa Clara County has filed a lawsuit against Meta, alleging the company failed to regulate scam advertisements on its platforms. This adds to the mounting legal challenges faced by Meta, following a significant ruling against the social media giant.

events.title: India and UAE sign defense pacts amidst Iran war tensions
events.desc: India and the United Arab Emirates have signed defense agreements focusing on maritime security, cyber defense, and military collaboration. This occurred as regional tensions remain high due to the ongoing war involving Iran.

events.title: Satellite imagery shows ongoing demolitions in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Satellite imagery reveals extensive destruction in southern Lebanon, with at least 46 out of 54 surveyed towns and villages heavily damaged or flattened. This comes despite a fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah.

events.title: Destruction at DRC mines under M23 control
events.desc: At least four landslides have been reported at the Rubaya mines in the Democratic Republic of Congo since the start of 2026, killing hundreds. The mines are currently under the control of the M23 group.

events.title: Efforts to destroy Iran's police infrastructure tracked
events.desc: Bellingcat has identified over 80 police stations and related infrastructure damaged or destroyed in Iran during the first three weeks of conflict with the US and Israel. Experts suggest the aim is to degrade the regime's repressive capacity.

events.title: Tool for damage assessment in Iran and the Gulf
events.desc: A new tool has been developed to assess damage in the conflict zones of Iran and the Gulf, particularly useful as access to open-source visuals is sporadic due to communication blackouts. This tool helps analyze satellite imagery to document destruction.

events.title: UAE downplays successful Iranian strikes
events.desc: Authorities in the United Arab Emirates have allegedly downplayed damage and misrepresented interceptions of Iranian drone strikes. Bellingcat's review indicates official statements do not always align with open-source evidence.

events.title: Pro-Gaza activist faces deportation push
events.desc: Lawyers for a pro-Palestinian activist are seeking to terminate deportation proceedings, alleging the Trump administration orchestrated the case. The activist was detained as part of a crackdown on foreigners linked to pro-Palestinian activities.

events.title: Israeli strikes wound dozens in Lebanon
events.desc: New Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon targeting Hezbollah wounded 37 people. This occurred as Lebanese and Israeli envoys began their second day of peace talks in Washington, with the UN condemning the civilian toll.

events.title: UAE to fast-track second oil pipeline
events.desc: The UAE is prioritizing the completion of a second oil pipeline designed to bypass the Strait of Hormuz. This project aims to double oil exports via the Gulf of Oman and ensure supply continuity amid regional conflicts.

events.title: Netanyahu claims secret UAE visit, sparking fury
events.desc: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu publicly revealed a secret visit to the UAE, causing embarrassment and anger in Abu Dhabi. This occurred as UAE-Israeli cooperation against Iran was reportedly deepening.

events.title: Ceasefire holds but demolitions continue in Lebanon
events.desc: Although the ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah is largely holding, satellite imagery confirms ongoing demolitions in southern Lebanon. Significant damage has been reported in numerous towns and villages.

events.title: 'Floating armoury' ship seized by Iran in Gulf of Oman
events.desc: Reports indicate that a vessel described as a 'floating armoury' has been seized by Iranian military personnel in the Gulf of Oman. This incident adds to the maritime security concerns in the region.

events.title: Hezbollah support remains strong in south Lebanon despite ceasefire failures
events.desc: Despite ongoing Israeli attacks and occupation, many residents in southern Lebanon continue to support Hezbollah, viewing the group as the primary force capable of defending them. This sentiment persists even as the ceasefire struggles to fully halt hostilities.

events.title: Netanyahu confirms 'secret visit' to UAE during Iran war
events.desc: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu stated he secretly visited the UAE during the conflict with Iran. The UAE initially denied reports of the visit, emphasizing that relations were not based on 'unofficial arrangements'.

events.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 22, including children
events.desc: At least 22 people, including eight children, were killed in southern Lebanon following a series of Israeli air strikes. The health ministry reported the casualties.

events.title: Lebanon reports 13 killed in Israeli strikes, including paramedics
events.desc: Lebanon's health ministry reported that 13 people were killed in Israeli strikes in Nabatieh, southern Lebanon. Among the casualties were two paramedics who were on a rescue mission.

events.title: UK pledges air and naval assets for Strait of Hormuz defence
events.desc: The United Kingdom has committed to providing jets, drones, and a warship to support the defence mission in the Strait of Hormuz. This initiative involves over 40 participating nations and is set to commence when conditions permit.

events.title: Trump calls Iran ceasefire 'on massive life support'
events.desc: US President Trump described the current ceasefire with Iran as being 'on massive life support', criticizing Iran's counteroffer to end the war as 'unbelievably weak'. The month-long ceasefire appears to be critically endangered.

events.title: EU imposes sanctions on Israeli settlers over West Bank violence
events.desc: The European Union has agreed to implement sanctions targeting Israeli settlers responsible for violence in the West Bank. The EU's foreign policy chief stated that 'extremism and violence carry consequences,' a move Israel has branded as 'arbitrary'.

events.title: Trump calls Iran's response to peace proposal 'totally unacceptable'
events.desc: US President Trump has rejected Iran's counteroffer to end the war, deeming it 'totally unacceptable.' Iran's reported demands include lifting the US naval blockade, recognition of its sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, and compensation for war damages.

events.title: Lebanon reports 39 killed in Israeli attacks
events.desc: Lebanon has reported that 39 people were killed in recent Israeli attacks. Hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah continue despite a ceasefire agreement announced last month.
